usage_breakdown.label: Heating & hot water
tips: Lower thermostat by 1°C – saves ~6%
Insulate pipes and use timer on water heater
Shorter showers save hot water energy

usage_breakdown.label: Kitchen (cooking, fridge, dishwasher)
tips: Use lid on pots – cooks faster, uses less energy
Defrost fridge regularly
Run dishwasher only when full

usage_breakdown.label: Electronics & standby
tips: Unplug devices on standby – can save €50–100/year
Use power strips with switches

usage_breakdown.label: Lighting
tips: Switch to LED bulbs – 80% less energy
Turn off lights in unused rooms

usage_breakdown.label: Washing & drying
tips: Wash at 30°C instead of 60°C
Air-dry instead of tumble dryer when possible

usage_breakdown.label: Cooling & ventilation
tips: Close blinds during hot days
Use fans instead of AC where possible

usage_breakdown.label: Other
tips: Review all appliances for energy rating

usage_breakdown.label: EV charging & mobility
tips: Charge during off-peak hours
Consider solar-compatible charger if you move to a house

recommendation.why_explanation: We recommend a 2.0 kWp system because your estimated consumption is about 240 kWh/year, your roof profile (pitched south) appears suitable for roughly 2,240 kWh/year of production, and this size balances self-consumption with export for your goals (space heating). We have not assumed a battery unless your goal requires it.
why_recommend: **Space heating** → Heat pump + PV + smart energy management
A 2.0 kWp system targets your annual usage and daytime consumption pattern.
Annual CO₂ reduction of 0.86 tonnes.

usage_breakdown.label: Heating & hot water
tips: Lower thermostat by 1°C – saves ~6%
Insulate pipes and use timer on water heater
Shorter showers save hot water energy

usage_breakdown.label: Kitchen (cooking, fridge, dishwasher)
tips: Use lid on pots – cooks faster, uses less energy
Defrost fridge regularly
Run dishwasher only when full

usage_breakdown.label: Electronics & standby
tips: Unplug devices on standby – can save €50–100/year
Use power strips with switches

usage_breakdown.label: Lighting
tips: Switch to LED bulbs – 80% less energy
Turn off lights in unused rooms

usage_breakdown.label: Washing & drying
tips: Wash at 30°C instead of 60°C
Air-dry instead of tumble dryer when possible

usage_breakdown.label: Cooling & ventilation
tips: Close blinds during hot days
Use fans instead of AC where possible

usage_breakdown.label: Other
tips: Review all appliances for energy rating

usage_breakdown.label: EV charging & mobility
tips: Charge during off-peak hours
Consider solar-compatible charger if you move to a house

recommendation.why_explanation: We recommend a 2.0 kWp system because your estimated consumption is about 240 kWh/year, your roof profile (pitched south) appears suitable for roughly 2,240 kWh/year of production, and this size balances self-consumption with export for your goals (space heating). We have not assumed a battery unless your goal requires it.
why_recommend: **Space heating** → Heat pump + PV + smart energy management
A 2.0 kWp system targets your annual usage and daytime consumption pattern.
Annual CO₂ reduction of 0.86 tonnes.

assumptions: Location: 84347, Pfarrkirchen, Landkreis Rottal-Inn, Bayern, Deutschland
Battery replacement assumed at year 12 (65% of original cost)
Inverter replacement assumed at year 15 (35% of original cost)
Production estimates from PVGIS via backend API (not browser-direct)
PVGIS terrain-based horizon modelling does not account for nearby trees, chimneys, or neighbouring buildings. A site survey is needed for final accuracy.
This is an estimate, not a guaranteed return. Your final result depends on roof conditions, shading, electricity usage, installed equipment, grid approval, and final installer quote.

usage_breakdown.label: Heating & hot water
tips: Lower thermostat by 1°C – saves ~6%
Insulate pipes and use timer on water heater
Shorter showers save hot water energy

usage_breakdown.label: Kitchen (cooking, fridge, dishwasher)
tips: Use lid on pots – cooks faster, uses less energy
Defrost fridge regularly
Run dishwasher only when full

usage_breakdown.label: Electronics & standby
tips: Unplug devices on standby – can save €50–100/year
Use power strips with switches

usage_breakdown.label: Lighting
tips: Switch to LED bulbs – 80% less energy
Turn off lights in unused rooms

usage_breakdown.label: Washing & drying
tips: Wash at 30°C instead of 60°C
Air-dry instead of tumble dryer when possible

usage_breakdown.label: Cooling & ventilation
tips: Close blinds during hot days
Use fans instead of AC where possible

usage_breakdown.label: Other
tips: Review all appliances for energy rating

usage_breakdown.label: EV charging & mobility
tips: Charge during off-peak hours
Consider solar-compatible charger if you move to a house

recommendation.why_explanation: We recommend a 2.0 kWp system because your estimated consumption is about 5,812 kWh/year, your roof profile (pitched south) appears suitable for roughly 3,173 kWh/year of production, and this size balances self-consumption with export for your goals (space heating). We have not assumed a battery unless your goal requires it.
why_recommend: **Space heating** → Heat pump + PV + smart energy management
A 2.0 kWp system targets your annual usage and daytime consumption pattern.
Annual CO₂ reduction of 1.22 tonnes.
